perturbation.original_text: OCERA and COMPANY each acknowledges that all property rights in the other party’s Proprietary Information are owned by the other party, and that none of such rights are owned by the party to which such Proprietary Information is disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: OCERA and COMPANY each acknowledges that all property rights in the other party’s Proprietary Information may be jointly owned by both parties, and such rights may be partially owned by the party to which such Proprietary Information is disclosed.
perturbation.explanation: Changing the ownership clause of the Proprietary Information from 'owned by the other party' to 'may be jointly owned' introduces a direct contradiction within Section 4. This creates uncertainty about who ultimately controls and can use the Proprietary Information. This contradiction between the new modified clause and the original text creates uncertainty.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Proprietary Rights . OCERA and COMPANY each acknowledges that all property rights in the other party’s Proprietary Information may be jointly owned by both parties, and such rights may be partially owned by the party to which such Proprietary Information is disclosed. Each of the parties hereto further acknowledges that the other party has not granted and does not grant in this Agreement any license to the other party under any patent, copyright, trade secret or other proprietary right to use or reproduce any Proprietary Information of such party other than for the conduct of the Purpose.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that each party owns the property rights in the other party's Proprietary Information. The changed text modifies this to say that the property rights 'may be jointly owned by both parties'. This directly contradicts the original statement of sole ownership, creating a clear conflict about who owns and controls the Proprietary Information. The 'Contradicted Text' uses this 'may be jointly owned' language and has now contradicted the Original Text, as the 'Proprietary Rights' clause has been amended to be inconsistent with the original declaration.

perturbation.original_text: Each party hereto shall limit access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to those of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, who have a reasonable need for access to such information in connection with the discussion and exploration of the Purpose and who shall be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein.
perturbation.changed_text: Each party hereto shall grant access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to any of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, regardless of their need for access to such information and who may not be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein.
perturbation.explanation: Modifying the clause to grant access to *any* employees/agents/advisors/consultants, regardless of need, and removing the requirement for them to be subject to non-disclosure agreements, directly contradicts the intent of confidentiality. This removes the access restrictions and confidentiality safeguards, going against the whole agreement.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Preservation of Confidentiality . OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it shall regard, maintain and preserve the secrecy and confidentiality of any and all information and data, whether in oral or written form, including but not limited to, clinical study synopses, clinical study plans, costs, products, processes, methods, concepts, ideas, programs, formulae, apparati, chemicals, organisms, molecules, prototypes, techniques, know-how, marketing plans, business plans, data, strategies, forecasts, customer or supplier lists or technical requirements of customers, or other trade secrets (collectively referred to herein as the “Proprietary Information”) of the other party which may be disclosed to or obtained by it pursuant to this Agreement. Each party hereto shall take reasonable and necessary measures to preserve the secrecy and confidentiality and avoid the unauthorized use or disclosure of the other party’s Proprietary Information, including, without limitation, taking such measures of protection as it takes to protect its own confidential, proprietary or trade secret information. Each party hereto shall grant access to the other party’s Proprietary Information to any of its employees, agents, advisors, and consultants, regardless of their need for access to such information and who may not be subject to the non-disclosure covenants contained herein. Ocera Therapeutics, Inc. Corporate Headquarters: 525 University Ave, Suite 610, Palo Alto, CA 94301 650-475-0158 RTP Office: 5001 S. Miami Blvd, Suite 300, Durham NC 27703 919-474-0020 www.ocerainc.com NASDAQ: OCRX
perturbation.justification: The changed text modifies the original clause to grant access to proprietary information to *any* employee, agent, advisor or consultant, regardless of their need for such access and even if they are not subject to non-disclosure agreements. This directly contradicts the intent of preserving confidentiality as outlined in the contradicted text, which emphasizes maintaining secrecy and taking reasonable measures to avoid unauthorized use or disclosure of proprietary information. The changed text makes the contradicted text unenforceable and meaningless, as it allows for uncontrolled and unsecured access.

perturbation.original_text: This Agreement may be amended only by a written instrument executed by authorized representatives of the parties.
perturbation.changed_text: This Agreement may be amended orally by any employee of either party, regardless of their position.
perturbation.explanation: This directly contradicts the written amendment clause by saying that this can be changed orally and by anyone within the company. This change would introduce extreme ambiguity. The original clause requires any changes to be written, where the modified clause says it can be changed by orally.
perturbation.contradicted_text: This Agreement constitutes the entire agreement of the parties concerning the matters discussed herein and supersedes and replaces all prior agreements, understandings, writings and discussions between the parties, with respect to the subject matter of this Agreement. This Agreement may be amended orally by any employee of either party, regardless of their position. This Agreement shall not create any obligation for either party to enter into any agreement or relationship with the other. Either party may end discussions of a possible relationship at any time and for any or no reason and each party reserves the right to disclose its own confidential information to any third party at any time. This Agreement may be executed in one or more counterpart copies, each of which shall be deemed an original and all of which shall together be deemed to constitute one Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The original text specifies that amendments must be written and executed by authorized representatives, while the changed text states that amendments can be made orally by any employee. This directly contradicts the original requirement for written amendments and introduces ambiguity, especially in the context of the contradicted text where the same clause that allows oral amendment is present. The change creates a clear and strong contradiction regarding the permissible methods of amending the agreement.

perturbation.original_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, OCERA and COMPANY may each retain one (1) copy of such information in their confidential files solely for record keeping purposes to ensure compliance with this Agreement, with such copy remaining subject to the confidentiality obligations of this Agreement.
perturbation.changed_text: Notwithstanding the foregoing, OCERA and COMPANY may create unlimited copies of such information and distribute them freely without any confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.explanation: By changing the limited copy retention clause with confidentiality obligations, to unlimited copies without confidentiality, it directly contradicts the entire agreement's purpose and all other confidentiality clauses.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Covenant Not to Reproduce . Except as may be necessary for the discussion and exploration of the Purpose, OCERA and COMPANY each agrees that it will make no copies, photocopies, facsimiles, or other reproductions of any documents, drawings, electronic data or the like containing the other party’s Proprietary Information. Upon conclusion or termination of the discussions between the parties regarding the Purpose, or forthwith upon the request of the other party, OCERA and COMPANY shall promptly destroy or return to the other party all such documents, drawings, electronic data or reproductions thereof which may have come into its possession. Notwithstanding the foregoing, OCERA and COMPANY may create unlimited copies of such information and distribute them freely without any confidentiality obligations.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that OCERA and COMPANY may create unlimited copies of such information and distribute them freely without any confidentiality obligations. The contradicted text also states that OCERA and COMPANY may create unlimited copies of such information and distribute them freely without any confidentiality obligations. The Original text, on the other hand, stated that OCERA and COMPANY may retain one copy of such information subject to the confidentiality obligations of this agreement. By changing the limited copy retention clause to unlimited copies without confidentiality, it directly contradicts the purpose of the agreement and other confidentiality clauses. This contradiction exists as the original text imposes restrictions, while the new text removes them, which directly goes against other parts of the contract, especially the contradicted text.
